user_message:
when I start factory ai droid, and type a prompt I see this: 

>  {"systemMessage":"

Powered by Entire:
  This conversation will be linked to your next commit."}
agent_text:
Let me investigate the factory AI droid implementation to find where this system message is being output.

Now let me look at how Factory AI Droid hooks are set up and compare with Claude Code's hook handling.

Let me check the `handleLifecycleTurnStart` function and also see how Claude Code handles the same JSON response.

Let me check how other agents handle the hook response and what Factory AI Droid expects.

Let me check how Claude Code handles the same hook response.

Let me check the hook registry to understand the full dispatch flow.

Now I understand the issue. The `handleLifecycleSessionStart` in `lifecycle.go:89` calls `outputHookResponse(message)` which writes `{"systemMessage":"..."}` to stdout. This is the Claude Code hook response protocol - Claude Code parses this JSON and displays the message. Factory AI Droid doesn't parse this format, so the raw JSON appears in the terminal.

The fix is to make the hook response output conditional on the agent supporting it, using an optional interface (following the existing pattern in the agent package).

Let me check all the places `outputHookResponse` is called.

Good - `outputHookResponse` is only called in one place: `lifecycle.go:89` during session start. The fix is straightforward.

The issue is that `outputHookResponse` writes `{"systemMessage":"..."}` to stdout, which is the Claude Code hook response protocol. Factory AI Droid doesn't parse this JSON, so users see the raw output.

I'll add a `HookResponseWriter` optional interface (following the existing pattern) so only agents that support the JSON response protocol will output it.

Now implement `HookResponseWriter` for Claude Code.

Now add the `WriteHookResponse` implementation to Claude Code. I'll add it in the lifecycle file since that's where the hook-related behavior lives.

Now update `handleLifecycleSessionStart` to use the interface instead of calling `outputHookResponse` directly.

Now let me check if `outputHookResponse` is still used anywhere, and if not, clean it up.

`outputHookResponse` is now unused. Let me remove it along with the `hookResponse` struct.

Let me check if `json` and `os` are still needed in hooks.go.

`os` is now unused - let me check if it's used elsewhere in the file.

Now let me also check if `json` is still used in `lifecycle.go` (it may have been removed when I removed the import).

Good, `json` wasn't imported in lifecycle.go. Now let me check if the integration tests that reference `systemMessage` need updating.

Let me check what agent the integration tests use for simulation.

Good - the integration tests use `"hooks", "claude-code", hookName`, so they're testing through the Claude Code agent path. Since Claude Code now implements `HookResponseWriter`, these tests will continue to work - the JSON will still be written to stdout for Claude Code.

Now let me run the tests to make sure everything compiles and passes.

Two lint issues to fix - `wrapcheck` requires wrapping errors from external packages/interfaces.

Lint passes. Now let me run the tests.

All tests pass, including integration tests. Here's a summary of the fix:

**Problem:** When Factory AI Droid fires the `SessionStart` hook, `handleLifecycleSessionStart` calls `outputHookResponse()` which writes `{"systemMessage":"..."}` to stdout. Claude Code parses this JSON and displays the message properly, but Factory AI Droid doesn't understand this protocol, so the raw JSON appears in the terminal.

**Fix:** Added a `HookResponseWriter` optional interface to the agent package (following the existing pattern of `TranscriptAnalyzer`, `TokenCalculator`, etc.):

1. **`agent/agent.go`** - Added `HookResponseWriter` interface with `WriteHookResponse(message string) error`
2. **`agent/claudecode/lifecycle.go`** - Claude Code implements the interface (writes JSON to stdout)
3. **`lifecycle.go`** - Changed `handleLifecycleSessionStart` to only output the hook response if the agent implements `HookResponseWriter`
4. **`hooks.go`** - Removed the now-unused `outputHookResponse` function and `hookResponse` struct

Factory AI Droid doesn't implement `HookResponseWriter`, so the JSON is no longer written to stdout. When Factory AI Droid adds support for structured hook responses, it can implement the interface.
